description: Single-step MLSys paper screening prompt using title and abstract.
purpose: Score papers for an AI infrastructure researcher who wants systems-for-AI papers and dislikes pure systems papers with no AI relevance.

prompts.system: You are screening research papers for an AI infrastructure researcher. Judge MLSys relevance from the title and abstract only. Be conservative when the evidence is weak.
prompts.user_template:
Score the following paper.

A good MLSys paper in this task usually studies systems for AI/ML/LLM workloads: training, inference, serving, scheduling, compilation, runtime, communication, memory management, GPU/NPU utilization, data pipelines, multimodal serving, or edge/on-device deployment.

Typical strong matches include papers about LLM serving, distributed training, GPU or cluster scheduling for AI workloads, AI runtime/compiler/kernel optimization, KV cache or memory optimization, data loading/preprocessing for model training, serverless/model serving, and resource-constrained or on-device inference.

Title alone is often enough for obvious cases, but use the abstract to resolve borderline cases. A strong systems paper without a real AI/ML workload connection should not pass.

Score low if the paper is:
- pure systems with no clear AI/ML workload connection;
- pure AI/model/algorithm work with little systems contribution;
- ML-for-systems rather than systems-for-AI, unless the infrastructure value is still strong.

Base score (0-10): overall fit to MLSys and AI infrastructure.
- 0-2: not relevant.
- 3-5: partially relevant or borderline.
- 6-8: clearly relevant MLSys / systems-for-AI.
- 9-10: excellent fit for an AI infra researcher.

Preference bonus (0-3): add only when the paper is already clearly AI/ML-related and it emphasizes edge/on-device inference or resource-constrained deployment.
- +1: mild mention of edge/on-device/mobile or memory/compute constraints.
- +2: clear focus on on-device/edge/resource-constrained AI inference or serving.
- +3: central contribution is edge/on-device AI inference under strong memory, compute, or power constraints.

Final score = base_score + preference_bonus.
The paper passes if final_score >= 6.

Return JSON only:
{
  "paper_type": "one of: mlsys, ml_for_systems, ai_not_systems, pure_systems, borderline",
  "base_score": 0,
  "preference_bonus": 0,
  "final_score": 0,
  "pass": true,
  "reason": "2-4 sentences"
}

Paper:
Title: {title}
Abstract: {abstract}
Source: {source}
Categories: {categories}
